# oms-capture — LLM Reference

Captures a real OMS failure as a structured training scenario. Turns live incidents into permanent guards that run on every /oms-train pass.

## Triggers — use /oms-capture when

- CEO stops a task mid-way to correct routing or tier classification
- Trainer produces lesson_candidates with channel: "scenario"
- CEO overrides the synthesis decision post-delivery
- An agent produced a structurally wrong output (wrong format, wrong tier behavior, wrong scope)

## Invocation

```
/oms-capture                   # analyze current/recent task log, draft scenario
/oms-capture <task-id>         # capture from a specific task log
```

## What it does

1. Reads the task log for the failing task
2. Identifies the failure point: which agent, which step, what went wrong
3. Drafts a training scenario (YAML format matching agents/training/scenarios/ schema)
4. CEO reviews the scenario and acceptance criteria
5. Writes to agents/training/scenarios/NNN-<slug>.md
6. /oms-train picks it up on next run

## Output format

Scenario file at: ~/.claude/agents/training/scenarios/NNN-<slug>.md
Schema: ~/.claude/agents/training/index.md

## What it doesn't do

- Never modifies the failing agent persona directly — that's /oms-train's job
- Never auto-runs the training — CEO confirms scenario before it enters rotation
